CONTENT.Club career: Sosa is a youth academy graduate of Nacional. He made his professional debut for the club on 7 April 2021 in the second leg of 2020 Championship playoff final against Rentistas. Four days later, on 11 April, he joined Rentistas on a season long loan deal. He joined Liverpool Montevideo on a season long loan deal in February 2022.On 17 August 2022, Sosa signed with Bologna in Italy. On 4 September 2023, he joined Dinamo Zagreb on a season long deal with a buy-out option.
CONTENT.International career: Sosa was born in Uruguay and is of Italian descent, holding dual citizenship. He is a former Uruguayan youth international. He was included in the national team for the 2017 South American U-15 Championship and 2019 South American U-17 Championship.

SUMMARY: Enzo Joaquín Sosa Romañuk (born 10 January 2002) is a Uruguayan professional footballer who plays as a defender for Major League Soccer club CF Montréal, on loan from Serie A side Bologna.
